channel.title: 8bitconcepts Research
channel.description: Independent research on enterprise AI strategy, governance, and operational risk.

item.title: Your AI Is Moving Back Onto the Machine
item.description: The future of AI inference is not cloud versus device. The shift is hierarchy: cloud for frontier work, devices for the everyday intelligence layer close to private context.

item.title: The Agentic Accountability Gap
item.description: Why governance frameworks built for generative AI fail for agentic systems.

item.title: Shift Handoff Intelligence
item.description: Maintaining context and knowledge across AI system transitions and human-agent handoffs.

item.title: Beyond the Prompt
item.description: Moving past prompt engineering to systematic AI integration.

item.title: The Six Percent
item.description: The small fraction of organizations getting real value from AI — and what they do differently.

item.title: The Integration Tax
item.description: Hidden costs of integrating AI into existing workflows and systems.

item.title: The Mandate Trap
item.description: Top-down AI mandates vs. bottom-up adoption — which actually drives results.

item.title: The Org Chart Problem
item.description: Why organizational structure determines AI adoption outcomes.

item.title: The Measurement Problem
item.description: How enterprises measure AI ROI and where standard metrics fail.

item.title: The Hallucination Budget
item.description: Quantifying the cost of AI hallucinations and mitigation strategies for production systems.

item.title: The Guardrails Gap
item.description: Why enterprise AI safety frameworks fail when agents act autonomously.

item.title: Q2 2026 AI Engineering Hiring Snapshot
item.description: Live market data: 8,618 AI/ML engineering roles across 513 companies, $213k median, 599 new this week. OpenAI leads with 336 open roles.

item.title: Q2 2026 MCP Ecosystem Health
item.description: Live audit: 5,578 agent-ready sites indexed, only 575 (10.3%) pass a live JSON-RPC handshake. Category breakdown, newly-verified servers, and the regulated verticals still waiting to be built.

item.title: Q2 2026 AI Engineering Compensation by Skill
item.description: Research roles pay a $42k premium over generative-AI roles ($274k vs $231k avg), even though generative-AI has 2.5x more openings. Top-paying skill tags, most in-demand tags, sweet-spot skills, salary distribution across 3,402 salary-disclosed roles.

item.title: Q2 2026 Remote vs Onsite AI Hiring
item.description: Live workplace data: hybrid AI/ML roles pay a ~$35k premium over remote+onsite ($253k vs $218k). 55% of AI engineering still requires full onsite attendance. Onsite-heavy and remote-friendly companies, hybrid-premium analysis.

item.title: Q2 2026 The Junior AI Hiring Gap
item.description: Live experience-level data: only ~7% of AI/ML engineering roles are open to juniors. For every entry-level role, ~10 senior-plus roles -- the tightest junior-to-senior ratio in tech. Why the squeeze exists, companies still hiring juniors, and the career-switcher playbook.

item.title: The Self-Testing Layer
item.description: A researched white paper on why agentic businesses need self-testing, self-improving systems: artifact scoring, feedback loops, evaluator calibration, audit trails, and regression infrastructure.
